title: artemisia sawanensis
intro: Artemisia sawanensis, also known as Thai mugwort, is a perennial herb native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its small yellow flowers and bitter, aromatic leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve menstrual cramps, and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Thai mugwort include its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and soothe digestive issues. Thai mugwort can be prepared as a tea, capsule, or infusion, and is often used in conjunction with other herbs in traditional herbal remedies.
